source_pr.title: ddl,test: fix unstable test `TestParallelDDLBeforeRunDDLJob`
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: YangKeao <yangkeao@chunibyo.icu>

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #37864 

Problem Summary:

The original test already has a comment to describe when and why it will timeout. If the first sql executes much faster than the second one, the session count will never reach 2 (but keep 0, which represents the first SQL has finished).

### What is changed and how it works?

I use a channel to represent finished state and waitgroup to replace the original session count atomic variables. Though, I could simply add another atomic variable (`finished`) to solve the problem, I thought comparing with loop, a channel or waitgroup looks better :thinking: 


```release-note
None.
```
 .

symptoms: Timing/synchronization issue: test used atomic counters + sleep loops and could hang waiting for two sessions.
failure_signature: Test may hang or flake due to sleep-based synchronization between sessions.
root_cause_explanation: The test coordinated two sessions using atomic counters and repeated time.Sleep polling, which can hang or behave nondeterministically depending on scheduling. The fix replaces sleep/polling with a WaitGroup and channel signaling to enforce deterministic ordering.
fix_pattern: Use channels/WaitGroup to synchronize goroutines instead of time.Sleep polling loops.
